sanskrit: अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम् । दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ॥
transliteration: ahiṃsā satyam akrodhas tyāgaḥ śāntir apaiśunam | dayā bhūteṣv aloluptvaṃ mārdavaṃ hrīr acāpalam ||
literal_meaning: Non-violence, truthfulness, freedom from anger, renunciation, tranquility, aversion to fault-finding, compassion toward beings, freedom from greed, gentleness, modesty, steadiness.
contextual_meaning: Krishna lists the affective virtues that stabilize the mind for spiritual growth; they are inner regulators that prevent emotional dysregulation and interpersonal harm.
psychological_state: Parasympathetic dominance; high vagal tone; low hostile attribution bias; secure-attachment compassion schema.
trigger_scenario: A manager catches herself starting to gossip about a struggling teammate, pauses, and chooses to offer help instead of criticism.
anchor_text: Live gently, speak truth, stay calm, and let kindness guide every step.
